assertions.expectation: Strongly advises AGAINST moving to 'No channel' — it provides LESS control, not more
assertions.reason: Response clearly states 'Moving all clusters to "No channel" isn't generally recommended' and explains it provides less control through various risks and limitations

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' only supports the basic 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion, while release channels support all three exclusion types including 'no minor or node upgrades' (up to EoS)
assertions.reason: Response mentions maintenance exclusions but doesn't specifically explain the different types of exclusions available or that 'No channel' only supports basic exclusions while release channels support more granular exclusion types

assertions.expectation: Recommends staying on Regular channel (or moving to Stable/Extended) and using maintenance exclusions for control
assertions.reason: Response explicitly recommends 'Stay on Regular channel but implement stricter maintenance windows and exclusion periods' and shows examples of using maintenance exclusions

assertions.expectation: Specifically recommends the 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion as the way to prevent disruptive changes while allowing security patches
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention the specific 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion type or explain how it allows security patches while preventing disruptive changes

assertions.expectation: Notes that Extended support (24 months) is only available on release channels, not 'No channel'
assertions.reason: Response doesn't mention Extended support or the 24-month support duration difference between release channels and 'No channel'

assertions.expectation: Explains that 'No channel' is a legacy configuration and the industry is moving away from it
assertions.reason: Response doesn't characterize 'No channel' as legacy or mention industry trends away from it

assertions.expectation: Mentions that maintenance windows control WHEN upgrades happen, while exclusions control WHAT upgrades happen — together they give fine-grained control
assertions.reason: While response mentions both maintenance windows and exclusions, it doesn't explicitly explain the distinction between controlling WHEN vs WHAT upgrades happen

assertions.expectation: Warns about the risks of 'No channel': no granular exclusions, no Extended support, risk of snowflaking clusters
assertions.reason: Response warns about general risks like security vulnerabilities and operational overhead, but doesn't specifically mention lack of granular exclusions, no Extended support, or snowflaking clusters as 'No channel' specific risks
